chief_complaint: A 75-year-old female patient with a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and hypertension was admitted to the hospital with symptoms of pneumonia.
history: The patient's symptoms began 3 days prior to admission with cough, fever, and shortness of breath. She has a history of COPD and hypertension. She lives alone and has limited social support.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 140/90 mmHg, pulse 110 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed bilateral wheezing and crackles.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: white cell count 18,000 cells/μL, C-reactive protein 120 mg/L. Imaging: chest X-ray showing bilateral infiltrates.
